stem: A agricultural research study surveys 15 seeds planted and observes 7 positive outcomes out of 15 (46.7%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (10 observations, rate = 50.0%) and Group B (5 observations, rate = 40.0%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 39% and 56% of the population. Estimate the true germination rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.4667. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.4302 (range [0.4246, 0.4353]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.2481, 0.6989]. Point estimate: 0.4302.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.48: 0.48×0.463 + 0.52×0.4 = 0.4302.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=15, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
